annotations.label: Normal distribution

anchor.snippet: a normal distribution or Gaussian distribution is a type of continuous probability distribution for a real-valued random variable

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `gaussianReal μ v` as the Gaussian measure on ℝ with mean μ and variance v.

annotations.label: Central limit theorem (informal)

anchor.snippet: It states that the average of many statistically independent samples

annotations.note: Mathlib has the 1D CLT for i.i.d. variables converging in distribution to a Gaussian.

annotations.label: Linear combination of normals is normal

anchor.snippet: any linear combination of a fixed collection of independent normal deviates is a normal deviate

annotations.note: Sum of independent Gaussians and scalar multiplication are formalized; a single n-ary linear-combination statement is not present but follows by combining `gaussianReal_const_mul` and the independence-sum lemma.

annotations.label: Standard normal distribution

anchor.snippet: The simplest case of a normal distribution is known as the standard normal distribution or unit normal distribution

annotations.note: `gaussianReal 0 1` is the standard normal; `stdGaussian E` is the multivariate analogue.

annotations.label: Gauss's standard normal

anchor.snippet: Carl Friedrich Gauss , for example, once defined the standard normal as

annotations.note: Gauss's historical variance-1/2 convention is not separately formalized.

annotations.label: Stigler's standard normal

anchor.snippet: Stephen Stigler once defined the standard normal as

annotations.note: Stigler's alternative standardization is not formalized.

anchor.snippet: If ⁠ ⁠ is a standard normal deviate , then

annotations.note: Combined with `gaussianReal_const_add`, this gives that `σ·X + μ` is `gaussianReal μ σ²` when `X ~ gaussianReal 0 1`.

annotations.label: Standardization of a normal variate

anchor.snippet: Conversely, if ⁠ ⁠ is a normal deviate with parameters

annotations.note: Combining `gaussianReal_sub_const` with `gaussianReal_div_const` gives `(X-μ)/σ ~ gaussianReal 0 1`.

annotations.label: Notation for normal distribution

anchor.snippet: The normal distribution is often referred to as

annotations.note: Mathlib uses `gaussianReal μ v` rather than the customary `N(μ, σ²)` notation.

annotations.label: Precision parameterization

anchor.snippet: Some authors advocate using the precision

annotations.note: Mathlib only parameterizes Gaussians by mean and variance.

annotations.label: Alternative precision (reciprocal of standard deviation)

anchor.snippet: Alternatively, the reciprocal of the standard deviation

annotations.note: Reciprocal-standard-deviation parameterization is not present.

annotations.label: Normal as exponential family

anchor.snippet: Normal distributions form an exponential family with natural parameters

annotations.note: Mathlib has no formal `ExponentialFamily` class.

annotations.label: Standard normal CDF

anchor.snippet: The cumulative distribution function (CDF) of the standard normal distribution

annotations.note: Mathlib has no dedicated definition of Φ; one can take `gaussianReal 0 1 (Set.Iic x)` ad hoc.

annotations.label: Error function

anchor.snippet: The related error function

annotations.note: The error function `erf` is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Relation between Phi and erf

anchor.snippet: The two functions are closely related

annotations.note: Without erf or Φ formalized, the relation is absent.

annotations.label: General normal CDF

anchor.snippet: For a generic normal distribution with density

annotations.note: Mathlib expresses Gaussian measure of a set as an integral of the pdf but does not name the CDF.

annotations.label: Probability of interval

anchor.snippet: The probability that x lies between a and b

annotations.note: The interval-probability formula follows from `gaussianReal_apply_eq_integral` but is not stated directly.

annotations.label: Q-function

anchor.snippet: is often called the Q-function , especially in engineering texts

annotations.note: The tail-probability Q-function is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Rotational symmetry of Phi

anchor.snippet: has 2-fold rotational symmetry around the point (0,1/2)

annotations.note: No symmetry lemma for the standard normal CDF is present.

annotations.label: Asymptotic expansion of CDF

anchor.snippet: An asymptotic expansion of the cumulative distribution function for large x can be derived using integration by parts

annotations.note: Tail asymptotics of the normal CDF are not formalized.

annotations.label: Taylor series for normal density

anchor.snippet: The Taylor series for the normal distribution

annotations.note: The explicit Taylor expansion of the Gaussian pdf is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Taylor series for CDF

anchor.snippet: This series can be integrated term by term to obtain the Taylor series for the cumulative distribution function

annotations.note: No Taylor expansion of Φ is formalized.

annotations.label: Recursive Taylor expansion via Hermite polynomials

anchor.snippet: The recurrence relation for Hermite polynomials

annotations.note: Mathlib has the identity expressing iterated derivatives of the Gaussian via Hermite polynomials, but no Taylor-series recursion for Φ.

annotations.label: 68-95-99.7 rule

anchor.snippet: This is known as the 68–95–99.7 (empirical) rule

annotations.note: The empirical 68-95-99.7 rule is not stated.

annotations.label: Quantile/probit function

anchor.snippet: The quantile function of a distribution is the inverse of the cumulative distribution function

annotations.note: No probit/quantile function for the normal is defined.

annotations.label: Newton's method for quantile

anchor.section: Using root finding to compute the quantile function
anchor.snippet: can be used with Newton's method

annotations.note: Newton iteration applied to the normal quantile is not formalized.

annotations.label: Maximum entropy characterization of normal

anchor.snippet: It is also the continuous distribution with the maximum entropy for a specified mean and variance

annotations.note: Differential entropy and its maximum-entropy characterization are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Geary's characterization

anchor.snippet: Geary has shown, assuming that the mean and variance are finite

annotations.note: Geary's theorem on independence of sample mean and variance is not formalized.

annotations.label: Symmetries and derivatives of normal density

anchor.snippet: has the following properties: It is symmetric around the point

annotations.note: The Gaussian pdf and its basic properties (positivity, integrability) are formalized but symmetry/derivative properties are not stated as named lemmas.

annotations.label: Properties of standard normal density

anchor.snippet: the density ⁠ ⁠ of the standard normal distribution

annotations.note: Properties at μ=0, v=1 are specializations of the general `gaussianPDFReal` facts.

annotations.label: Plain and absolute moments

anchor.snippet: The plain and absolute moments of a variable

annotations.note: Mathlib has generic `moment` and `centralMoment`, but no specific definitions of plain/absolute moments for the normal.

annotations.label: Central moments of normal

anchor.snippet: the non-central moments exist and are finite for any

annotations.note: Finiteness of all moments of the Gaussian is captured by `memLp_id_gaussianReal`; closed-form moment formulas are not stated.

annotations.label: Central absolute moments

anchor.snippet: The central absolute moments coincide with plain moments for all even orders

annotations.note: Closed-form expressions for absolute moments are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Conditional expectation on an interval (inverse Mills ratio)

anchor.snippet: The expectation of ⁠ ⁠ conditioned on the event

annotations.note: The inverse Mills ratio is not formalized.

annotations.label: Fourier transform of normal density

anchor.snippet: The Fourier transform of a normal density

annotations.note: Mathlib computes the Fourier integral of a Gaussian.

annotations.label: Characteristic function

anchor.snippet: is closely connected to the characteristic function

annotations.note: Mathlib has the general `charFun` and the closed form for the Gaussian.

annotations.label: Moment generating function of normal

anchor.snippet: the moment generating function exists and is equal to

annotations.note: Mathlib proves `mgf X p t = exp(μt + v t²/2)` for Gaussian `X`.

annotations.label: Cumulant generating function

anchor.snippet: The cumulant generating function is the logarithm of the moment generating function

annotations.note: Both the generic `cgf` and the Gaussian formula `μt + v t²/2` are present.

annotations.label: Stein operator and class

anchor.snippet: Within Stein's method the Stein operator and class of a random variable

annotations.note: Stein's method is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Zero-variance limit (Dirac delta)

anchor.snippet: In the limit when

annotations.note: Mathlib defines the zero-variance Gaussian as `Measure.dirac μ`.

annotations.label: Maximum entropy theorem for normal

anchor.snippet: Of all probability distributions over the reals with a specified finite mean

annotations.note: Differential entropy and its maximization are not formalized.

annotations.label: Marcinkiewicz theorem

anchor.snippet: the Marcinkiewicz theorem (named after Józef Marcinkiewicz ) asserts that

annotations.note: Marcinkiewicz's theorem on characteristic functions is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Jointly normal and uncorrelated implies independent

anchor.snippet: are jointly normal and uncorrelated , then they are independent

annotations.note: Mathlib proves zero covariance implies independence for jointly Gaussian random variables.

annotations.label: Kullback-Leibler divergence of normals

anchor.snippet: The Kullback–Leibler divergence of one normal distribution

annotations.note: KL divergence between measures is defined generally as `InformationTheory.klDiv`, but the closed-form formula for two normals is not stated.

annotations.label: Hellinger distance between normals

anchor.snippet: The Hellinger distance between the same distributions is equal to

annotations.note: The Hellinger distance and its closed form for normals are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fisher information matrix

anchor.snippet: The Fisher information matrix for a normal distribution

annotations.note: Fisher information is not formalized.

annotations.label: Conjugate prior for the mean

anchor.snippet: The conjugate prior of the mean of a normal distribution is another normal distribution

annotations.note: Bayesian conjugate-prior theory is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Statistical manifold of normals

anchor.snippet: In information geometry , the family of normal distributions forms a statistical manifold with constant curvature

annotations.note: Information geometry / statistical manifolds are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Central limit theorem

anchor.snippet: The central limit theorem states that under certain (fairly common) conditions

annotations.note: 1D CLT for i.i.d. variables with finite variance is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Berry-Esseen theorem

anchor.snippet: A general upper bound for the approximation error in the central limit theorem is given by the Berry–Esseen theorem

annotations.note: No Berry-Esseen bound is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Closure of normal family under linear transformations

anchor.section: Operations on a single normal variable
anchor.snippet: the family of normal distributions is closed under linear transformations

annotations.note: Mathlib proves a linear map of a `gaussianReal` is again `gaussianReal`, with a multivariate analogue in `IsGaussian.isGaussian_map`.

annotations.label: Mean equals median and mode

annotations.label: Gaussian is an eigenfunction of the Fourier transform

annotations.note: The eigenfunction property is an immediate consequence of the general Fourier-of-Gaussian formula, but no specialized eigenfunction lemma is stated.
